bullets: ["Stress triggers the release of cortisol which directly increases oil production, weakens the skin barrier and slows healing. The skin and stress connection is physiological, not imagined.","Poor sleep compounds this. Your skin does the majority of its repair work overnight and consistently disrupted sleep shows on the skin faster than almost any other lifestyle factor.","You cannot always control your stress levels but you can track them. Your daily log is capturing this data and over time patterns will emerge that show you exactly how stress affects your specific skin.","Managing stress is skincare. It belongs in your routine as much as your cleanser does."]

tip_title: Getting the most from your SPF
bullets: ["Apply SPF as the very last step of your morning routine, after moisturiser.","Use enough. Most people apply far too little. A full teaspoon for face and neck.","Reapply every two hours if you are outdoors or in direct sunlight.","SPF in makeup or moisturiser alone is not sufficient. Coverage is never even enough."]

["Dairy and high glycaemic foods are the most common dietary triggers for acne and inflammation. You do not need to eliminate them entirely but noticing patterns is worthwhile.","Omega-3 fatty acids found in oily fish, walnuts and flaxseed actively support skin barrier function and reduce inflammation.","Alcohol dehydrates the skin and disrupts sleep quality. Both show on your face faster than most people expect.","No single food will fix your skin but a consistently anti-inflammatory diet supports everything else you are doing."]

["Less is more with active ingredients. A pea-sized amount of serum is enough for the whole face.","Applying more does not make a product work faster. It increases the risk of irritation.","Give each product thirty seconds to absorb before applying the next.","Pat products in gently rather than rubbing. Your skin barrier will thank you."]

["Hormonal fluctuations throughout the month directly affect sebum production, skin sensitivity and healing speed. Your skin is not being unpredictable. It is responding to your cycle.","The week before your period is typically when skin is most reactive, oiliest and most prone to breakouts. This is the week to simplify your routine and avoid introducing anything new.","Tracking your cycle alongside your skin helps identify your personal patterns. Over time this data becomes one of the most useful things AIDA can show you.","If you suspect a hormonal condition such as PCOS is affecting your skin it is worth discussing with your GP. Skincare can support but it cannot override a hormonal imbalance that needs medical attention."]

tip_title: After your facial — the next 24 hours
bullets: ["Avoid touching your face unnecessarily. Your skin is in repair mode and needs to be left alone.","No makeup for at least 12 hours if possible. Let your skin breathe and absorb everything it has received.","Stay out of direct sunlight and skip any heat treatments such as saunas or steam rooms for 24 hours.","Some clients experience a light purge in the week following a deep treatment facial. This is a normal part of the clearing process and will pass.","Drink plenty of water. Hydration supports everything your skin is doing right now."]

["Always introduce one new active ingredient at a time, never two at once, so you know exactly what is working or what is reacting.","Start slowly. Use two to three times a week before building to daily, and always patch test first on a small area for three days.","Active ingredients like retinol, vitamin C, acids and exfoliators are powerful but they need to earn their place in your routine gradually.","Never use retinol and a strong acid on the same evening. They could destabilise each other and irritate the skin barrier. If you are ever unsure about combining ingredients, get in touch and we will guide you."]

tip_title: What to expect in the first few weeks
bullets: ["Your skin may feel more reactive before it fully settles. This is completely normal when your routine is adjusting and does not mean the products are not working.","Stick with what has been recommended and resist introducing anything new for at least four weeks. Less is genuinely more during this period.","If you experience persistent irritation or anything that concerns you before your next appointment, get in touch. You are never bothering us.","Give your skin the same patience you would give anything worth having. Real change takes time but it is coming."]

["Change your pillowcase at least twice a week. Your skin spends eight hours pressed against it every night.","Clean your phone screen daily. It carries more bacteria than most surfaces you touch.","Wash your hands before applying skincare. Introducing bacteria to your face undoes the work of your routine.","Use a clean towel for your face, separate from your body towel, and change it every two to three days."]
